seoTitle: Fix and Enable Your Camera in Microsoft Teams
metaDescription: Turn on your webcam in Microsoft Teams from the pre-join screen or an active call, and fix common black-screen and permission errors.
intro: A black video tile right before an important call is one of the most common Teams frustrations, and it's usually a quick fix once you know where the toggles live. This guide walks through turning on your camera before joining, switching it mid-call, and resolving the operating system permission blocks that most often cause the problem.

steps.heading: Turn on your camera before joining
steps.body: On the pre-join preview screen, slide the camera toggle to on and confirm your video appears in the thumbnail before entering the meeting.

steps.heading: Check your device settings
steps.body: Open the device settings drawer to switch between a built-in webcam and an external HD camera if your feed looks wrong or the wrong device is selected.

steps.heading: Toggle your camera mid-meeting
steps.body: Once inside a call, use the camera icon on the top toolbar to turn video on or off at any time.

steps.heading: Fix operating system permission blocks
steps.body: If the picture stays black, open your Windows or macOS privacy settings and confirm Teams has been granted camera access.

steps.heading: Polish your presentation
steps.body: Apply a background blur or virtual backdrop and check the preview thumbnail to confirm good framing and lighting before you go live.

faq.question: Why is my Teams camera showing a black screen even though it's enabled?
faq.answer: This is usually an operating system privacy block — check Windows or macOS camera permissions and make sure Teams is allowed.

faq.question: Can I use an external webcam instead of my laptop's built-in camera?
faq.answer: Yes, the device settings menu lets you select any connected external camera as your active video source.

faq.question: Does the browser version of Teams need separate camera permission?
faq.answer: Yes, browser-based Teams requires its own camera and microphone permission prompt separate from the desktop app.

faq.question: Can I test my camera before joining a call?
faq.answer: Yes, the pre-join preview screen shows a live thumbnail so you can confirm your camera works before entering the meeting.
